name: Carburetor Suppression
description: Carburetor Suppression is a conspiracy theory primarily circulating in the United States, alleging that major automotive manufacturers, oil companies, and potentially government entities are actively suppressing the development and implementation of more efficient carburetor technologies. Proponents believe that superior carburetor designs, capable of significantly increasing fuel efficiency and reducing emissions, have been deliberately shelved to protect the profits of powerful corporations invested in the existing gasoline-powered engine infrastructure. These suppressed carburetors are often claimed to achieve mileage figures far exceeding current industry standards, sometimes reaching hundreds of miles per gallon. The origins of the theory can be traced back to the energy crises of the 1970s and growing environmental awareness, fueling suspicion about the motivations of large corporations. Mainstream science and engineering generally dismiss these claims, citing fundamental principles of thermodynamics and fluid dynamics that limit the potential efficiency gains from carburetor modifications. They argue that while carburetor technology has evolved, the core limitations inherent in the design prevent the dramatic improvements claimed by proponents. Furthermore, they point to the widespread adoption of fuel injection systems as a more efficient and controllable method of fuel delivery, rendering carburetors obsolete rather than suppressed. The theory persists due to distrust in large corporations and government, a desire for technological solutions to environmental problems, and the allure of a simple fix to complex issues.
subtitle: Claimed plot to hide efficient tech

claims.claim: High-mileage carburetors have been invented and suppressed.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts, patents acquired and never developed, claims of inventors being silenced or bought out.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence, violation of fundamental physics principles, more efficient technology (fuel injection) already exists.

claims.claim: Oil companies and automakers conspire to maintain dependence on gasoline.
claims.evidence: Historical examples of corporations prioritizing profits over public good, lobbying efforts against alternative energy sources.
claims.criticism: Exaggerated interpretation of corporate behavior, ignores market forces driving technological development, cost and complexity of large-scale conspiracy.

claims.claim: Government regulatory agencies are complicit in suppressing carburetor technology.
claims.evidence: Slow approval processes for innovative technologies, alleged collusion between government and industry.
claims.criticism: Standard regulatory procedures, focus on safety and emissions standards, lack of incentive for government to suppress beneficial technology.

keyFigures.name: Tom Ogle
keyFigures.role: Inventor of the 'Ogle Carburetor,' a claimed high-mileage design.

keyFigures.name: Ricardo Pielke Jr.
keyFigures.role: Science Policy Expert who has written about the perpetuation of this theory.

mythologyConnections: Prometheus (bringing fire/technology to humanity and facing consequences)
David and Goliath (small inventor vs. powerful corporation)

timeline.event: Energy crisis fuels distrust in oil companies and interest in alternative fuel technologies.
Tom Ogle demonstrates his 'Ogle Carburetor', generating significant media attention.
The theory persists online in forums and alternative media outlets.

sources.author: Jeane Manning
sources.work: Energy Technologies: Towards a Sustainable Future
sources.title: Jeane Manning

sources.author: Mike Brown
sources.work: Fuel Injection: Installation, Performance Tuning, Modification
sources.title: Mike Brown

sources.author: Ricardo Pielke Jr.
sources.work: The Honest Broker: Making Sense of Science in Policy and Politics
sources.title: Ricardo Pielke Jr.

worshipPractices.name: The Ritual of Re-Jetting
worshipPractices.description: A clandestine ceremony performed in garages and workshops, involving the modification of existing carburetors to achieve increased efficiency. Participants believe this act defies the forces of Carburetor Suppression, unlocking hidden potential within the engine.

worshipPractices.name: The Vigil of the Blocked Patent
worshipPractices.description: An annual observation where followers gather to remember and discuss patents for advanced carburetor technologies that they believe were deliberately suppressed. They share stories of inventors silenced and innovations lost, renewing their commitment to the cause.

festivals.name: The Combustion Jubilee
festivals.timing: Held annually on the anniversary of the alleged initial act of Carburetor Suppression.
festivals.description: A celebration featuring DIY demonstrations of carburetor modifications, sharing of fuel-saving tips, and passionate debates about the conspiracy. Participants often display vintage vehicles with modified carburetors as symbols of defiance.

festivals.name: The Rally for Revelation
festivals.timing: Held sporadically, often coinciding with major automotive industry events.
festivals.description: A protest rally where believers attempt to raise awareness about Carburetor Suppression and demand the release of suppressed technologies. They often carry signs and banners displaying slogans related to fuel efficiency and corporate accountability.

cultural.modernLegacy: The myth of Carburetor Suppression persists as a symbol of distrust towards corporations and governments, particularly in the context of environmental concerns and technological advancement. It fuels a subculture of tinkering and DIY innovation focused on sustainable solutions, reflecting a desire for greater control over technology and resources.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Just as Carburetor Suppression posits the suppression of a technology, the binding of Fenrir represents the suppression of a powerful force perceived as a threat to the established order of the gods. Both involve a perceived danger being contained or prevented from reaching its full potential.

Pandora's Box, though different in origin, can be seen as a parallel in that it represents the suppression (initially) and then release of something that has profound consequences. The "something" suppressed in the myth is the technological advancement of the carburetor.

Apep, the serpent of chaos, is constantly being battled and confined by Ra. This mirrors the idea of Carburetor Suppression in that a perceived threat (a superior technology disrupting the status quo) is actively being controlled or limited by powerful forces.

associations.name: Pandora's Box
associations.significance: Represents the unintended consequences and unforeseen problems that can arise from suppressing knowledge or innovation, as opening the box unleashed evils upon the world.

associations.name: Ouroboros
associations.significance: Symbolizes the self-defeating nature of suppressing progress, as the snake eating its own tail suggests that hindering innovation ultimately harms the system itself.

associations.name: Quicksilver (Mercury)
associations.significance: Represents the elusive nature of the suppressed technology, always shifting and difficult to grasp, mirroring the alchemical pursuit of elusive transformations.

associations.name: Seven
associations.significance: Associated with hidden knowledge and secrets in many mythologies, suggesting that the suppressed carburetor technology is part of a larger, concealed truth.

associations.name: Eclipse
associations.significance: Symbolizes the obscuring or blocking of knowledge and progress, much like an eclipse temporarily hides the sun's light.

associations.name: Mandrake
associations.significance: Represents the dangerous knowledge that, when unearthed (or suppressed), can have unforeseen and potentially harmful consequences.

associations.name: Shadow
associations.significance: The unknown, the unseen, and the secrets that are hidden from the world, thus representing the suppression.

companions.name: Prometheus
companions.description: The Titan who gifted fire to humanity, symbolizing the pursuit of knowledge and technology against the will of powerful entities. He would align with those seeking to liberate suppressed innovations.

companions.name: Daedalus
companions.description: The master craftsman and inventor, known for his ingenuity and ability to overcome seemingly impossible challenges. He represents the innovative spirit stifled by Carburetor Suppression.

companions.name: Sleipnir
companions.description: Odin's eight-legged horse, symbolizing speed and overcoming obstacles. Represents the advanced technology held back by the conspiracy.

companions.name: The Morrigan
companions.description: The Celtic goddess of war and fate, she would protect those fighting for truth and justice against powerful, unseen forces. Her foresight would aid in uncovering the conspiracy.

companions.name: Hermes/Mercury
companions.description: The messenger god, swift and knowledgeable, facilitating the spread of information and exposing the conspiracy. He would assist in disseminating the truth about suppressed carburetor technology.

extendedContent.title: The Myth of Perpetual Motion Denied: A Modern Prometheus?
extendedContent.content: Carburetor Suppression, at its core, echoes the ancient human desire for perpetual motion and limitless resources, a theme consistently present in mythology across cultures. The idea that a simple, readily available technology could provide a near-limitless supply of fuel efficiency mirrors the myth of the philosopher's stone, capable of transmuting base metals into gold, or the cornucopia, an unending source of sustenance. The alleged suppression of this technology casts automotive manufacturers and oil companies in the role of modern-day titans, hoarding power and resources for themselves, much like the gods of Olympus who feared humanity's potential. The inventors and proponents of these 'superior' carburetors become Promethean figures, stealing fire (fuel efficiency) from the gods (corporations) and incurring their wrath. This narrative taps into deep-seated anxieties about corporate greed, technological control, and the perceived imbalance of power between individuals and large institutions. This is a modern, secular manifestation of ancient anxieties surrounding access to vital resources and the potential for those in power to control them for their own benefit, even at the expense of the common good. The idea of a hidden, superior technology also plays on the Gnostic concept of hidden knowledge that can liberate humanity from material constraints and suffering, offering a form of secular salvation through technological advancement.

extendedContent.title: The Serpent in the Engine: Conspiracy as Creation Myth
extendedContent.content: The Carburetor Suppression myth can be interpreted as a reverse creation myth. Instead of explaining the origin of a desirable element (like fire or agriculture), it explains the lack of something perceived as essential – readily available, highly efficient fuel technology. The 'serpent' in this garden of automotive technology is the conspiracy itself, whispering doubts about the official narrative and suggesting a hidden truth. The fall from grace isn't a historical event, but a continuous state of being, perpetrated by those who control the flow of resources. The 'forbidden fruit' is the knowledge of this suppressed technology, knowledge that could liberate individuals from dependence on expensive and environmentally damaging fuels. This narrative creates a sense of shared victimhood among believers, uniting them against a common enemy. The act of believing in the Carburetor Suppression myth becomes a form of rebellion, a way to reclaim agency and challenge the established order. It offers a framework for understanding perceived economic and environmental injustices, transforming frustration and powerlessness into a narrative of resistance. This modern mythology provides a way for people to interpret the world and assert their belief in a system that actively deprives them of resources they believe they deserve.

extendedContent.title: The Echoes of Lost Technologies: Atlantis and the Free Energy Narrative
extendedContent.content: The belief in Carburetor Suppression shares striking similarities with other 'lost technology' myths, such as the legend of Atlantis and its advanced energy sources or the more modern narratives surrounding free energy devices. These myths often depict a golden age of technological advancement, followed by a catastrophic event or deliberate suppression by those in power, leading to the loss of this knowledge. The narrative of Atlantis, for example, often includes advanced energy systems that were either destroyed or deliberately hidden from humanity. Similarly, proponents of free energy devices often claim that their inventions are suppressed by the energy industry. Carburetor Suppression fits neatly into this pattern, offering a specific and seemingly plausible example of a readily available technology that has been unjustly denied to the public. This resonates with a deep-seated human longing for a utopian future powered by clean and abundant energy, a future that is always just out of reach. The appeal lies in the promise of a world without resource scarcity, a world where technology serves humanity rather than the interests of a powerful few. This is a recurring theme in utopian and dystopian literature, and the Carburetor Suppression myth taps into the same anxieties and aspirations.

extendedContent.title: The Hero's Journey Reversed: From Inventor to Outcast
extendedContent.content: The narrative surrounding Carburetor Suppression often involves the figure of the lone inventor, the brilliant but persecuted genius who discovers a revolutionary technology only to be silenced and marginalized by powerful forces. This character arc is a distorted version of the classic hero's journey. Instead of being celebrated and rewarded for their innovation, the inventor is ostracized and discredited, becoming a victim of their own ingenuity. This resonates with stories of scientific pioneers who were initially ridiculed or ignored by the established scientific community, such as Galileo Galilei or Nikola Tesla. The Carburetor Suppression myth, however, amplifies this narrative, turning the inventor into a martyr for the cause of technological liberation. Their failure to bring their invention to the masses is not seen as a personal shortcoming, but as evidence of a vast and powerful conspiracy. This reinforces the belief that the system is rigged against innovation and that only by challenging the established order can true progress be achieved. It creates a sense of camaraderie among believers, who see themselves as supporting the underdog against overwhelming odds.

extendedContent.title: The Enduring Legacy: Hope, Suspicion, and the Search for Truth
extendedContent.content: The legacy of the Carburetor Suppression myth extends beyond its specific claims about fuel efficiency. It represents a broader cultural phenomenon of mistrust in institutions, particularly corporations and governments, and a desire for alternative narratives that challenge the status quo. It fuels a constant search for hidden truths and suppressed technologies, often intersecting with other conspiracy theories related to energy, health, and technology. Even if the specific claims of Carburetor Suppression are ultimately disproven, the underlying anxieties and desires that fuel the myth will likely persist. The myth serves as a cautionary tale about the potential for technological progress to be stifled by vested interests and the importance of critical thinking and independent inquiry. It highlights the tension between innovation, profit, and public good, and the challenges of ensuring that technological advancements benefit all of humanity rather than just a select few. The enduring legacy is a constant questioning of authority, a persistent search for alternative explanations, and a deep-seated hope that technological solutions can overcome societal problems, even in the face of powerful opposition. It is a potent reminder that myths, even those based on questionable evidence, can shape our understanding of the world and influence our actions.
